contentBlocks.original:
अगस्तिरुवाच
आजानुबाहुमरविन्ददलायताक्ष-
-माजन्मशुद्धरसहासमुखप्रसादम् ।
श्यामं गृहीत शरचापमुदाररूपं
रामं सराममभिराममनुस्मरामि ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
agastiruvācha
ājānubāhumaravindadaḻāyatākṣa-
-mājanmaśuddharasahāsamukhaprasādam ।
śyāmaṃ gṛhīta śarachāpamudārarūpaṃ
rāmaṃ sarāmamabhirāmamanusmarāmi ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Agastya said: I ever meditate on Rama — long-armed, with eyes like lotus petals, whose face radiates the smile of natural purity, dark-complexioned, holding bow and arrow, of noble form, together with Sita (Sa-Rama — with Sita), the enchanting Rama.

contentBlocks.original: अस्य श्रीरामकवचस्य अगस्त्य ऋषिः अनुष्टुप् छन्दः सीतालक्ष्मणोपेतः श्रीरामचन्द्रो देवता श्रीरामचन्द्रप्रसादसिद्ध्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः ।
contentBlocks.transliteration: asya śrīrāmakavachasya agastya ṛṣiḥ anuṣṭup Chandaḥ sītālakṣmaṇōpētaḥ śrīrāmachandrō dēvatā śrīrāmachandraprasādasiddhyarthē japē viniyōgaḥ ।
contentBlocks.translation: This is the Rama Kavacham: its seer is the sage Agastya, the metre is Anushtup, the deity is Sri Ramachandra with Sita and Lakshmana. It is employed for the attainment of Sri Ramachandra's grace.

contentBlocks.original:
अथ ध्यानं
नीलजीमूतसङ्काशं विद्युद्वर्णाम्बरावृतम् ।
कोमलाङ्गं विशालाक्षं युवानमतिसुन्दरम् ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
atha dhyānaṃ
nīlajīmūtasaṅkāśaṃ vidyudvarṇāmbarāvṛtam ।
kōmalāṅgaṃ viśālākṣaṃ yuvānamatisundaram ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Dhyana (meditation on Rama's form): Dark as a blue thundercloud, clothed in lightning-hued garments; of tender form, wide-eyed, young and surpassingly beautiful.

contentBlocks.original:
सीतासौमित्रिसहितं जटामुकुटधारिणम् ।
सासितूणधनुर्बाणपाणिं दानवमर्दनम् ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sītāsaumitrisahitaṃ jaṭāmukuṭadhāriṇam ।
sāsitūṇadhanurbāṇapāṇiṃ dānavamardanam ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Together with Sita and Lakshmana (Saumitri), wearing the crown of matted locks; holding bow, arrows and a quiver, the crusher of demons.

contentBlocks.original:
यदा चोरभये राजभये शत्रुभये तथा ।
ध्यात्वा रघुपतिं क्रुद्धं कालानलसमप्रभम् ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yadā chōrabhayē rājabhayē śatrubhayē tathā ।
dhyātvā raghupatiṃ kruddhaṃ kālānalasamaprabham ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: When there is fear of thieves, fear of the king, fear of enemies — then meditate on Raghupati (Rama) in his fierce aspect, blazing like the fire of dissolution.

contentBlocks.original:
चीरकृष्णाजिनधरं भस्मोद्धूलितविग्रहम् ।
आकर्णाकृष्टविशिखकोदण्डभुजमण्डितम् ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
chīrakṛṣṇājinadharaṃ bhasmōddhūḻitavigraham ।
ākarṇākṛṣṭaviśikhakōdaṇḍabhujamaṇḍitam ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Wearing tree-bark and black deer-skin, body smeared with ash; drawing the bow to his ear, adorned with the mighty Kodanda bow.

contentBlocks.original:
रणे रिपून् रावणादींस्तीक्ष्णमार्गणवृष्टिभिः ।
संहरन्तं महावीरमुग्रमैन्द्ररथस्थितम् ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
raṇē ripūn rāvaṇādīṃstīkṣṇamārgaṇavṛṣṭibhiḥ ।
saṃharantaṃ mahāvīramugramaindrarathasthitam ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Destroying enemies in battle — Ravana and others — with fierce showers of arrows; the great hero, the fierce one, standing on the chariot of Indra.

contentBlocks.original:
लक्ष्मणाद्यैर्महावीरैर्वृतं हनुमदादिभिः ।
सुग्रीवाद्यैर्माहावीरैः शैलवृक्षकरोद्यतैः ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
lakṣmaṇādyairmahāvīrairvṛtaṃ hanumadādibhiḥ ।
sugrīvādyairmāhāvīraiḥ śailavṛkṣakarōdyataiḥ ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Surrounded by the great heroes led by Lakshmana and Hanuman; and the great warriors led by Sugriva, raising rocks and trees.

contentBlocks.original:
वेगात्करालहुङ्कारैर्भुभुक्कारमहारवैः ।
नदद्भिः परिवादद्भिः समरे रावणं प्रति ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vēgātkarālahuṅkārairbhubhukkāramahāravaiḥ ।
nadadbhiḥ parivādadbhiḥ samarē rāvaṇaṃ prati ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: With swift and terrible war-cries and earth-shaking roars; shouting and counter-shouting against Ravana in the battle.

contentBlocks.original:
श्रीराम शत्रुसङ्घान्मे हन मर्दय खादय ।
भूतप्रेतपिशाचादीन् श्रीरामाशु विनाशय ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrīrāma śatrusaṅghānmē hana mardaya khādaya ।
bhūtaprētapiśāchādīn śrīrāmāśu vināśaya ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Srirama — strike, crush, devour my enemy hosts! O Srirama — swiftly destroy ghosts, spirits and demons!

contentBlocks.original:
एवं ध्यात्वा जपेद्रामकवचं सिद्धिदायकम् ।
सुतीक्ष्ण वज्रकवचं शृणु वक्ष्याम्यनुत्तमम् ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ēvaṃ dhyātvā japēdrāmakavachaṃ siddhidāyakam ।
sutīkṣṇa vajrakavachaṃ śṛṇu vakṣyāmyanuttamam ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Thus meditating, recite the Rama Kavacham that bestows all perfection. Listen — I shall now recite the supremely excellent, razor-sharp, vajra-armour.

contentBlocks.original:
अथ कवचम्
श्रीरामः पातु मे मूर्ध्नि पूर्वे च रघुवंशजः ।
दक्षिणे मे रघुवरः पश्चिमे पातु पावनः ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
atha kavacham
śrīrāmaḥ pātu mē mūrdhni pūrvē cha raghuvaṃśajaḥ ।
dakṣiṇē mē raghuvaraḥ paśchimē pātu pāvanaḥ ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Now the Kavacham begins: May Srirama protect my crown; may the son of the Raghu clan protect me in the east; may Raghuvara protect me in the south; may the purifier protect me in the west.

contentBlocks.original:
उत्तरे मे रघुपतिर्भालं दशरथात्मजः ।
भ्रुवोर्दूर्वादलश्यामस्तयोर्मध्ये जनार्दनः ॥ 11 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
uttarē mē raghupatirbhālaṃ daśarathātmajaḥ ।
bhruvōrdūrvādalaśyāmastayōrmadhyē janārdanaḥ ॥ 11 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: May Raghupati protect me in the north and my forehead; may Dasharatha's son protect me; may the dark one, gentle as durva grass, protect between the brows; may Janardana protect the space between them.

contentBlocks.original:
श्रोत्रं मे पातु राजेन्द्रो दृशौ राजीवलोचनः ।
घ्राणं मे पातु राजर्षिर्गण्डौ मे जानकीपतिः ॥ 12 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrōtraṃ mē pātu rājēndrō dṛśau rājīvalōchanaḥ ।
ghrāṇaṃ mē pātu rājarṣirgaṇḍau mē jānakīpatiḥ ॥ 12 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: May the king of kings protect my ears; may the lotus-eyed one protect my eyes; may the royal sage protect my nose; may Janaki's lord protect my cheeks.

contentBlocks.original:
कर्णमूले खरध्वंसी भालं मे रघुवल्लभः ।
जिह्वां मे वाक्पतिः पातु दन्तपङ्क्ती रघूत्तमः ॥ 13 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
karṇamūlē kharadhvaṃsī bhālaṃ mē raghuvallabhaḥ ।
jihvāṃ mē vākpatiḥ pātu dantapaṅktī raghūttamaḥ ॥ 13 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: May the destroyer of Khara protect the base of my ears; may Raghuvallabha protect my forehead; may the lord of speech protect my tongue; may the best of the Raghus protect my rows of teeth.

contentBlocks.original:
ओष्ठौ श्रीरामचन्द्रो मे मुखं पातु परात्परः ।
कण्ठं पातु जगद्वन्द्यः स्कन्धौ मे रावणान्तकः ॥ 14 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ōṣṭhau śrīrāmachandrō mē mukhaṃ pātu parātparaḥ ।
kaṇṭhaṃ pātu jagadvandyaḥ skandhau mē rāvaṇāntakaḥ ॥ 14 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: May Ramachandra protect my lips; may the beyond-the-beyond protect my mouth; may the one worshipped by the universe protect my throat; may the destroyer of Ravana protect my shoulders.

contentBlocks.original:
धनुर्बाणधरः पातु भुजौ मे वालिमर्दनः ।
सर्वाण्यङ्गुलिपर्वाणि हस्तौ मे राक्षसान्तकः ॥ 15 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dhanurbāṇadharaḥ pātu bhujau mē vālimardanaḥ ।
sarvāṇyaṅguliparvāṇi hastau mē rākṣasāntakaḥ ॥ 15 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: May the bow-and-arrow bearer protect my arms; may the crusher of Vali protect me; may the destroyer of rakshasas protect all my fingers and all joints of the hands.

contentBlocks.original:
वक्षो मे पातु काकुत्स्थः पातु मे हृदयं हरिः ।
स्तनौ सीतापतिः पातु पार्श्वं मे जगदीश्वरः ॥ 16 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vakṣō mē pātu kākutsthaḥ pātu mē hṛdayaṃ hariḥ ।
stanau sītāpatiḥ pātu pārśvaṃ mē jagadīśvaraḥ ॥ 16 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: May Kakutstha protect my chest; may Hari protect my heart; may Sitapati protect my breasts; may the lord of the universe protect my sides.

contentBlocks.original:
मध्यं मे पातु लक्ष्मीशो नाभिं मे रघुनायकः ।
कौसल्येयः कटी पातु पृष्ठं दुर्गतिनाशनः ॥ 17 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
madhyaṃ mē pātu lakṣmīśō nābhiṃ mē raghunāyakaḥ ।
kausalyēyaḥ kaṭī pātu pṛṣṭhaṃ durgatināśanaḥ ॥ 17 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: May the lord of Lakshmi protect my middle; may the leader of the Raghu clan protect my navel; may Kaushalyeya protect my waist; may the destroyer of misfortune protect my back.

contentBlocks.original:
गुह्यं पातु हृषीकेशः सक्थिनी सत्यविक्रमः ।
ऊरू शार्ङ्गधरः पातु जानुनी हनुमत्प्रियः ॥ 18 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
guhyaṃ pātu hṛṣīkēśaḥ sakthinī satyavikramaḥ ।
ūrū śārṅgadharaḥ pātu jānunī hanumatpriyaḥ ॥ 18 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: May Hrishikesha protect my private parts; may the truly valiant protect my thighs; may the bearer of the Sharnga bow protect my upper legs; may the beloved of Hanuman protect my knees.

contentBlocks.original:
जङ्घे पातु जगद्व्यापी पादौ मे ताटकान्तकः ।
सर्वाङ्गं पातु मे विष्णुः सर्वसन्धीननामयः ॥ 19 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jaṅghē pātu jagadvyāpī pādau mē tāṭakāntakaḥ ।
sarvāṅgaṃ pātu mē viṣṇuḥ sarvasandhīnanāmayaḥ ॥ 19 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: May the all-pervading one protect my calves; may the destroyer of Tataka protect my feet; may Vishnu protect all my limbs; may the blameless one protect all my joints.

contentBlocks.original:
ज्ञानेन्द्रियाणि प्राणादीन् पातु मे मधुसूदनः ।
पातु श्रीरामभद्रो मे शब्दादीन्विषयानपि ॥ 20 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jñānēndriyāṇi prāṇādīn pātu mē madhusūdanaḥ ।
pātu śrīrāmabhadrō mē śabdādīnviṣayānapi ॥ 20 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: May Madhusudana protect my organs of knowledge and my vital airs; may Sri Ramabhadra protect me and all my objects of the senses.

contentBlocks.original:
द्विपदादीनि भूतानि मत्सम्बन्धीनि यानि च ।
जामदग्न्यमहादर्पदलनः पातु तानि मे ॥ 21 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dvipadādīni bhūtāni matsambandhīni yāni cha ।
jāmadagnyamahādarpadalanaḥ pātu tāni mē ॥ 21 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Two-footed and other beings related to me; all deeds done knowingly or unknowingly.

contentBlocks.original:
सौमित्रिपूर्वजः पातु वागादीनीन्द्रियाणि च ।
रोमाङ्कुराण्यशेषाणि पातु सुग्रीवराज्यदः ॥ 22 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
saumitripūrvajaḥ pātu vāgādīnīndriyāṇi cha ।
rōmāṅkurāṇyaśēṣāṇi pātu sugrīvarājyadaḥ ॥ 22 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: May the destroyer of Parashurama's pride burn all those (sins) swiftly; may Rama, bearer of the Sharnga bow, protect me on all sides always.

contentBlocks.original:
वाङ्मनोबुद्ध्यहङ्कारैर्ज्ञानाज्ञानकृतानि च ।
जन्मान्तरकृतानीह पापानि विविधानि च ॥ 23 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vāṅmanōbuddhyahaṅkārairjñānājñānakṛtāni cha ।
janmāntarakṛtānīha pāpāni vividhāni cha ॥ 23 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: This kavacham of Sri Ramachandra is equal to the vajra armour — the most secret of all secrets, the divine — O best of ascetics, Sutikshna.

contentBlocks.original:
तानि सर्वाणि दग्ध्वाशु हरकोदण्डखण्डनः ।
पातु मां सर्वतो रामः शार्ङ्गबाणधरः सदा ॥ 24 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tāni sarvāṇi dagdhvāśu harakōdaṇḍakhaṇḍanaḥ ।
pātu māṃ sarvatō rāmaḥ śārṅgabāṇadharaḥ sadā ॥ 24 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Whoever reads or hears or causes others to hear this with concentrated mind — by the grace of Ramachandra, that person reaches the supreme state.

contentBlocks.original:
इति श्रीरामचन्द्रस्य कवचं वज्रसम्मितम् ।
गुह्याद्गुह्यतमं दिव्यं सुतीक्ष्ण मुनिसत्तम ॥ 25 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
iti śrīrāmachandrasya kavachaṃ vajrasammitam ।
guhyādguhyatamaṃ divyaṃ sutīkṣṇa munisattama ॥ 25 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Even one guilty of the gravest sins, even a cow-slayer, even a destroyer of embryos — by reciting this Rama Kavacham they shall be purified.

contentBlocks.original:
यः पठेच्छृणुयाद्वापि श्रावयेद्वा समाहितः ।
स याति परमं स्थानं रामचन्द्रप्रसादतः ॥ 26 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yaḥ paṭhēchChṛṇuyādvāpi śrāvayēdvā samāhitaḥ ।
sa yāti paramaṃ sthānaṃ rāmachandraprasādataḥ ॥ 26 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Even those guilty of brahmahatya (killing a Brahmin) are freed — there is no doubt. O Sutikshna, just as you asked me before — so I have narrated this Sri Rama Kavacham to you.

contentBlocks.original:
महापातकयुक्तो वा गोघ्नो वा भ्रूणहा तथा ।
श्रीरामचन्द्रकवचपठनाच्छुद्धिमाप्नुयात् ॥ 27 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mahāpātakayuktō vā gōghnō vā bhrūṇahā tathā ।
śrīrāmachandrakavachapaṭhanāchChuddhimāpnuyāt ॥ 27 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Even the gravest of sinners, even a cow-slayer or embryo-destroyer — by reciting the Sri Ramachandra kavacham they shall be purified.

contentBlocks.original:
ब्रह्महत्यादिभिः पापैर्मुच्यते नात्र संशयः ।
भो सुतीक्ष्ण यथा पृष्टं त्वया मम पुराः शुभम् ।
तथा श्रीरामकवचं मया ते विनिवेदितम् ॥ 28 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
brahmahatyādibhiḥ pāpairmuchyatē nātra saṃśayaḥ ।
bhō sutīkṣṇa yathā pṛṣṭaṃ tvayā mama purāḥ śubham ।
tathā śrīrāmakavachaṃ mayā tē vinivēditam ॥ 28 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Even the sin of killing a Brahmin is destroyed — there is no doubt. O Sutikshna, as you asked me — I have set forth this Sri Rama Kavacham.

contentBlocks.original: इति श्रीमदानन्दरामायणे मनोहरकाण्डे सुतीक्ष्णागस्त्यसंवादे श्रीरामकवचम् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration: iti śrīmadānandarāmāyaṇē manōharakāṇḍē sutīkṣṇāgastyasaṃvādē śrīrāmakavacham ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Thus the Sri Rama Kavacham from the dialogue of Sutikshna and Agastya in the Manohara Kanda of the Srimad Ananda Ramayana is complete.
